concept.conceptName: Carbon dioxide/Gas.total.at end expiration in Exhaled gas by Infrared absorption
Carbon dioxide/Gas.total.at end expiration in Exhaled gas

en.name: End tidal carbon dioxide fraction (FetCO2)
en.shortDescription: End-tidal CO2 as a volume fraction / concentration, in %. The partial-pressure (mmHg) form is a separate set.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

End-tidal carbon dioxide (EtCO2) is the carbon dioxide in the gas a patient exhales at the very end of a breath, measured by a capnograph. This set captures EtCO2 expressed as a **volume fraction / concentration**, reported in **%** (e.g. 5%). In source data it is a single numeric value per patient-timestamp, recorded in intensive care and anaesthesia for ventilated patients. (EtCO2 expressed as a partial pressure in mmHg/kPa is a separate, non-inter-convertible measurement — see Mapping Notes.)

## Included Concepts

- *3012287 / 19888-7 — Carbon dioxide/Gas.total.at end expiration in Exhaled gas by Infrared absorption (LOINC)*: by infrared capnography (the standard method).
- *3017485 / 19889-5 — Carbon dioxide/Gas.total.at end expiration in Exhaled gas (LOINC)*: method not specified.

**Note on units**: reported in **`8554 / % — percent`** (UCUM), the recommended unit.

## Mapping Notes

- Map an EtCO2 value reported as a percentage to one of these concepts.
- **Fraction vs partial pressure are not inter-convertible**: EtCO2 expressed as a partial pressure (PetCO2, mmHg/kPa) belongs to the separate **End tidal carbon dioxide (PetCO2)** concept set. The two are not related by a fixed factor (the partial pressure depends on barometric pressure), so they are kept as separate, unit-homogeneous sets.
- **Not arterial CO2**: arterial CO2 is a different measurement in its own concept set.

fr.name: Fraction télé-expiratoire de dioxyde de carbone (FetCO2)
fr.shortDescription: CO2 télé-expiratoire en fraction volumique / concentration, en %. La forme en pression partielle (mmHg) est un set distinct.
